title: New Trade Agreement Boosts Mongolia–EU Commerce
excerpt: ICC Mongolia celebrates the signing of a comprehensive bilateral trade agreement expected to increase exports by 30%.
body:
ICC Mongolia marked a historic milestone this week as Mongolia and the European Union officially signed a comprehensive bilateral trade agreement — the most significant trade deal Mongolia has entered into in over a decade.

The agreement, which covers goods, services, and intellectual property protections, is projected to increase Mongolian exports by 30% over the next five years. Key sectors expected to benefit include mining, cashmere, agriculture, and technology.

"This agreement is a testament to the tireless advocacy of ICC Mongolia and our members," said Lkhagvasuren Damdinsuren, President of ICC Mongolia. "We look forward to supporting Mongolian businesses in taking full advantage of this new access to European markets."

ICC Mongolia will host a series of workshops throughout April to help member companies understand the agreement's provisions and prepare for new export opportunities.

title: International Arbitration Seminar Draws Record Attendance
excerpt: Over 200 legal and business professionals gathered for our annual arbitration seminar at the State Palace.
body:
ICC Mongolia's annual International Arbitration Seminar broke attendance records this year, welcoming more than 200 legal professionals, business executives, and government officials to the State Palace of Culture in Ulaanbaatar.

The two-day event featured keynote presentations from leading arbitration experts from Singapore, Vienna, and Hong Kong, alongside panel discussions on the evolving landscape of dispute resolution in Central Asia.

Highlights included a session on the new ICC Arbitration Rules 2024 updates, a mock arbitration exercise, and a roundtable on enforcing arbitral awards in Mongolia under the New York Convention.

Secretary General Myadagmaa Jadambaa noted: "The growing interest in international arbitration reflects Mongolia's increasing integration into global trade. ICC Mongolia is committed to building the expertise and infrastructure needed to make Ulaanbaatar a regional arbitration hub."

title: ICC Mongolia Welcomes 50 New Corporate Members
excerpt: Our fastest-growing membership quarter on record reflects the Mongolian business community's desire for global connections.
body:
ICC Mongolia is proud to announce that 50 new corporate members joined our organisation in the first quarter of 2025 — the fastest membership growth in the organisation's history.

The new members span a wide range of industries including banking, mining, logistics, technology, and retail. Among the new members are several multinational companies establishing operations in Mongolia, as well as fast-growing Mongolian enterprises seeking access to international networks.

"This growth is a strong signal that Mongolian businesses understand the value of being connected to the world's largest business network," said President Lkhagvasuren Damdinsuren. "We welcome each new member and look forward to supporting their international ambitions."

New members gain access to ICC Mongolia's full suite of services including arbitration support, trade facilitation resources, policy advocacy, and exclusive networking events.

title: Incoterms® 2020 Workshop: Practical Guide for Exporters
excerpt: Join ICC Mongolia for a hands-on workshop on applying Incoterms® 2020 rules to your international trade contracts.
body:
ICC Mongolia is hosting a full-day practical workshop on Incoterms® 2020, designed specifically for Mongolian exporters, freight forwarders, and legal professionals involved in international trade contracts.

The workshop will cover all 11 Incoterms® rules with real-world examples drawn from Mongolia's key export sectors. Participants will learn how to select the correct Incoterm for different transport modes, allocate risk and cost correctly, and avoid common contractual errors that lead to disputes.

The session will be led by an ICC-certified Incoterms® trainer and conducted in both Mongolian and English.

Date: April 15, 2025
Venue: ICC Mongolia Office, ICC Tower, 11th floor
Registration: Contact info@icc-mongolia.org

title: ICC Mongolia Hosts Policy Forum on Sustainable Mining
excerpt: Business leaders and government officials convened to discuss responsible mining practices and international ESG standards.
body:
ICC Mongolia convened a high-level policy forum bringing together mining company executives, government ministers, and international experts to discuss the future of sustainable mining in Mongolia.

The forum examined how Mongolian mining companies can align with international Environmental, Social, and Governance (ESG) standards to attract foreign investment and meet growing demands from international partners for responsible sourcing.

Participants discussed the ICC Business Charter for Sustainable Development and explored how its principles can be adapted to Mongolia's regulatory environment.

The forum produced a joint statement calling for a national ESG framework for the mining sector, which ICC Mongolia will present to the Ministry of Mining and Heavy Industry in the coming weeks.

title: ICC Mongolia Annual Report 2024 Now Available
excerpt: Our 2024 Annual Report highlights key achievements in arbitration, trade facilitation, membership growth, and advocacy.
body:
ICC Mongolia is pleased to release its Annual Report for 2024, providing a comprehensive overview of the organisation's activities, achievements, and financial performance over the past year.

Key highlights from 2024 include:

  • 47 new arbitration cases filed under ICC Rules
  • Record membership growth with 180 active corporate members
  • 12 major policy submissions to government ministries
  • 18 training workshops and seminars conducted
  • Participation in 6 international ICC World Council sessions

The report also outlines ICC Mongolia's strategic priorities for 2025, including expanding arbitration services, deepening engagement with the ICC Digital Economy Commission, and launching a new SME membership category.

The full report is available for download on our website or in print at the ICC Mongolia office.

title: ICC Mongolia to Host Inaugural Youth Business Summit
excerpt: Young entrepreneurs and business students will gather for a one-day summit on innovation, trade, and global opportunities.
body:
ICC Mongolia is proud to announce its first-ever Youth Business Summit, scheduled for May 10, 2025 at the Shangri-La Hotel in Ulaanbaatar. The summit is designed to connect young Mongolian entrepreneurs and business students with international business leaders and ICC Mongolia member companies.

The programme includes keynote talks on innovation and global trade, a startup pitch competition, networking sessions, and workshops on accessing ICC resources as a young professional.

"The next generation of Mongolian business leaders deserves a platform to develop their skills and build connections early," said Secretary General Myadagmaa Jadambaa. "This summit is our investment in Mongolia's business future."

Registration is open to participants aged 18–35. Seats are limited. Contact info@icc-mongolia.org to register.

title: ICC Mongolia Releases Cashmere Export Outlook 2025
excerpt: A new report examines opportunities and barriers for Mongolian cashmere exporters in European and Asian markets.
body:
ICC Mongolia has published its Cashmere Export Outlook 2025, a comprehensive analysis of the global cashmere market and the opportunities available to Mongolian producers and exporters.

The report finds that demand for ethically sourced, premium Mongolian cashmere continues to grow in European luxury markets, while new opportunities are emerging in South Korea, Japan, and China's premium retail sector.

Key barriers identified include inconsistent grading standards, limited traceability infrastructure, and high logistics costs. The report recommends adopting internationally recognised quality certification and building direct trade relationships with luxury brands.

"Cashmere is one of Mongolia's most important export products and a real competitive advantage," noted Board Member Amartuvshin Hanibal. "This report gives our members the data they need to make strategic decisions."

The full report is available to ICC Mongolia members through the member portal.

title: ICC Mongolia Launches Digital Trade Facilitation Initiative
excerpt: A new programme will help Mongolian businesses adopt electronic trade documents and streamline cross-border procedures.
body:
ICC Mongolia has launched a new Digital Trade Facilitation Initiative aimed at helping Mongolian businesses transition from paper-based to electronic trade documentation, in line with the UN Model Law on Electronic Transferable Records (MLETR).

The initiative, supported by the ICC Digital Economy Commission, will provide training, technical resources, and advocacy to reduce friction in Mongolia's cross-border trade processes.

Phase one focuses on electronic bills of lading and letters of credit, targeting logistics companies and banks that handle high volumes of international trade documents.

"Digital trade documents can reduce transaction costs by up to 80% and processing times from days to hours," said President Lkhagvasuren Damdinsuren. "This is a critical step in modernising Mongolia's trade infrastructure."

title: ICC Mongolia Signs Partnership Agreement with ADB
excerpt: A new memorandum of understanding with the Asian Development Bank will expand access to financing for Mongolian SMEs.
body:
ICC Mongolia has signed a Memorandum of Understanding with the Asian Development Bank (ADB) to strengthen support for small and medium-sized enterprises (SMEs) in Mongolia seeking access to trade finance and international markets.

Under the agreement, ICC Mongolia will collaborate with ADB on capacity-building programmes, market intelligence reports, and facilitation of trade finance instruments including letters of credit and supply chain financing.

The partnership is expected to benefit hundreds of Mongolian SMEs over the next three years, particularly those in export-oriented sectors such as agriculture, manufacturing, and technology.

"Access to finance remains one of the biggest barriers for Mongolian SMEs going international," said Deputy Chairwoman Erdenechimeg. "This partnership with ADB directly addresses that gap."

title: ICC Mongolia Adopts Anti-Corruption Framework for Members
excerpt: Member companies are encouraged to adopt ICC's global anti-corruption tools to strengthen governance and investor confidence.
body:
ICC Mongolia has officially adopted the ICC Anti-Corruption Framework, a comprehensive set of tools and guidelines to help member companies implement effective anti-bribery and anti-corruption programmes in line with international standards.

The framework includes the ICC Rules on Combating Corruption, practical implementation guides, and access to ICC's global network of compliance experts. Member companies that adopt the framework will receive a certificate of participation.

"Strong governance and zero tolerance for corruption are essential for attracting the foreign investment Mongolia needs," said President Lkhagvasuren Damdinsuren. "We call on all our members to take this step."

ICC Mongolia will hold a dedicated workshop on implementing the framework in March 2025. Details will be announced shortly.

title: Trade Finance Masterclass Equips 80 Banking Professionals
excerpt: ICC Mongolia's intensive two-day masterclass on trade finance instruments trained professionals from 12 Mongolian banks.
body:
In December 2024, ICC Mongolia delivered a two-day Trade Finance Masterclass attended by 80 professionals from 12 Mongolian commercial banks and financial institutions.

The masterclass covered letters of credit, documentary collections, bank guarantees, and supply chain finance, with a focus on practical application of ICC's Uniform Customs and Practice for Documentary Credits (UCP 600) and Uniform Rules for Collections (URC 522).

Participants completed case studies based on real Mongolian trade scenarios and received ICC certificates of completion.

"Improving trade finance expertise in the banking sector is essential for supporting Mongolian exporters and importers," said Secretary General Myadagmaa Jadambaa. "We plan to run this masterclass annually."

The next Trade Finance Masterclass is scheduled for June 2025. Registrations will open in April.
